methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing planktonic foraminifer samples from Miocene sites for morphological analysis.

steps.description: Sites U1489 and U1490 both recovered the lower and middle Miocene and were chosen for the relatively good preservation of planktonic foraminifers.
steps.searchable_summary: select sites U1489 and U1490 for sample collection based on preservation quality

steps.description: Table T1 lists the samples analyzed, including their depth in the section and biostratigraphic age assignment.
steps.searchable_summary: list samples with depth and biostratigraphic age
outputs: sample list with depth and age

steps.description: Dentoglobigerina and Globoquadrina specimens were picked manually from the residues of the >150 μm size fraction using a light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: manually pick specimens from residues using a light microscope

steps.description: Images in three views (umbilical, edge, and spiral view) of the selected specimens were taken using an Olympus SZX10 z-stacking light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: capture images of specimens in three views using z-stacking light microscope

steps.description: A Jeol JSM-6480LV high-performance SEM from the UCL Earth Sciences laboratory was used to examine wall texture and assess the presence of morphological features, including spine holes and pustules, at higher resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: use SEM to examine wall texture and morphological features

steps.description: Each stub was sputter-coated in an argon and gold atmosphere using a sputter-coater machine.
steps.searchable_summary: sputter-coat stubs in argon and gold atmosphere

steps.description: Spine holes were investigated across the three views of each specimen's test, and the study focused on areas where pores were sufficiently preserved, pore ridges smooth, and not affected by diagenetic processes.
steps.searchable_summary: investigate spine holes and pore preservation in specimen tests
